title: Heptapleurum tanjiewhoeianum
intro: Heptapleurum tanjiewhoeianum, also known as Tanjiewhoe's heptapleurum, is a species of flowering plant in the Araliaceae family, native to the tropical forests of New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its purported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Heptapleurum tanjiewhoeianum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and lignans, which have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the production of tinctures and ointments.

This classification is based on the plant's morphology and molecular characteristics. The exact classification of Heptapleurum tanjiewhoeianum may be subject to further research and revision.
